narration: Every time you see a sale, your brain says you're winning. But here's the thing — science proves you're actually spending more, not less.
visual: Large orange price tag SVG with '40% OFF' springs in with rotation, text 'YOU THINK SALES SAVE MONEY' fades in below on dark background

narration: Seeing the word 'SALE' floods your brain's reward center with dopamine. It literally shuts off rational thinking — the same way gambling does.
visual: Simplified brain outline SVG on light background with glowing orange reward center pulsing, text 'DISCOUNTS HIJACK YOUR BRAIN'

narration: Here are the numbers: 72% of sale purchases were never on your shopping list. And the average shopper loses $800 a year on unplanned deals.
visual: Progress bar animating to 72% with counting percentage, then dollar counter climbing to $800, all on dark background

narration: When you see $100 crossed out with $60 underneath it, your brain doesn't feel poorer — it feels like you just earned $40. That's the trap.
visual: Two SVG price tags: gray $100 with red strikethrough, arrow pointing to orange $60 tag labeled 'SAVE $40!', on light background

narration: Retailers know this cold. They inflate prices up to 83% before the sale — so that 40% off was never a real discount at all.
visual: Bar chart: red bar rising to $140 'INFLATED', then orange bar at $84 'SALE' appears with spinning sale badge, dark background

narration: Beat the trap with three rules: make a list before you shop, wait 48 hours on any deal, and never browse without a goal. Save this.
visual: Checklist on light background with three rules, checkboxes animating in one by one with orange checkmarks, CTA text fades in at bottom
